meaning: n.流动性,流质,变移性,流度
examples: Cognitive fluidity explains the appearance of art, religion, and sophisticated speech.
She danced with great fluidity of movement.
It is generally intelligible and coherent, with some fluidity of expression, though it exhibits some noticeable lapses in the expression of ideas.
Dorsey sees that fluidity of financial exchange as a critical capability for cities of the future.
In general, though, fluidity of labor as well as capital is an economic plus.
